title: anaphalis margaritacea subsp. morrisonicola
intro: Anaphalis margaritacea subsp. morrisonicola is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the pearly everlasting. It is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Anaphalis margaritacea subsp. morrisonicola can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
